nfcu_ana_greeting.ai_message: Good morning, Derek. Your workforce forecast needs attention. Tax season volume surge is 6 days out and your current staffing plan has a 17-point coverage gap. I also see your new hire cohort is underperforming on AHT. Want me to walk you through the risks?

Show me the training cohort data

nfcu_ana_signal_1_tax_season.ai_message: The pattern is clear and the window is closing — 6 days before impact. That same playbook projected at today's numbers puts you at 79% service level for $11,200 — just under target but recoverable. I've pre-built three staffing scenarios. Want to start with the hybrid plan?

nfcu_ana_signal_2_training.ai_message: The root cause isn't knowledge — it's workflow adoption. Agents are bypassing the guided resolution flow and escalating to supervisors prematurely, which inflates AHT and loads the supervisor tier unnecessarily. Critical implication for tax season: do not assign this cohort to mortgage or auto loan queues during peak. They need 3 more weeks before they can handle high-complexity volume independently.

confidence.rationale: Process-adherence telemetry is authoritative; cohort-readiness window is anchored to historical n=8 cohort ramps with ±5-day variance.

The good news: the hybrid tax season plan I modeled stays within 96% of budget. Restructuring how mortgage servicing handles PTO coverage could recover $8,000–$10,000 in Q2 spend. Want to see the breakdown?

nfcu_ana_turn_1_tax_season.ai_message:
Based on 3 years of historical data, tax season drives a 45% increase in call volume, concentrated in general banking and mortgage servicing queues.

Peak impact: April 12–17.

Your current schedule: 48 agents across those queues during peak hours.
You need: 70 agents to maintain 80/20 service level.
Gap: **22 agents**.

Without intervention, projected service level: 62% — well below the 80% target.

Last year, you closed a 19-agent gap with 12 overtime shifts and 8 cross-trained agents from digital channels. That scenario cost $14,200 and achieved 81% service level.
suggested_chips: What are my options to close the gap?
Run a what-if with 15 overtime shifts
Show me which queues are most impacted
How did we handle it last year?

ui_components_to_render.title: Tax Season Call Volume: 2024 vs. 2025 vs. 2026 (Projected)

confidence.rationale: Three years of clean tax-season data; 45% volume lift is consistent (±4% YoY). Gap math is deterministic.

nfcu_ana_turn_2_what_if.ai_message:
Scenario modeled — 15 overtime shifts distributed across April 12–17:

**Projected service level:** 62% → **76%** (still 4 points below target)
**Overtime cost:** ~$18,400 → puts Q2 budget at **96%**

Adding callback option for waits over 3 minutes closes the remaining gap → **81% service level**.

**Alternative — Hybrid Option (recommended):**
8 overtime shifts + 10 cross-trained agents from digital channels.
• Service level projection: **79%** (within 1 point of target)
• Cost: **$11,200** (puts Q2 budget at **85%**)
• Frees $7,200 buffer for unplanned PTO

The hybrid option gives you 79% service level at 73% of the cost of 15 OT shifts.

nfcu_ana_turn_3_cohort.ai_message:
The March cohort of 12 agents is behind schedule. At week 6, their AHT is 14:30 vs. the 9:00 target.

**Root cause:** Knowledge base adherence is only 62%. Dynamics 365 process adherence reports show these agents skip the guided resolution flow 38% of the time — going directly to supervisor escalation instead.

Historically, cohorts that hit 85% KB adherence by week 4 reach AHT targets by week 6. This cohort may need **3 additional weeks** before they can handle peak volume independently.

**For tax season:** These agents should not be assigned to high-complexity queues (mortgage, auto loans). They can support digital support or general banking under closer supervision.

**Recommended:** Schedule targeted KB coaching this week. 3 agents are closest to the 85% threshold and could be ready by April 10.
suggested_chips: Can any of them handle tax season volume?

nfcu_ana_fourth_scenario.ai_message:
Modeled — **Scenario 4: Temporary Agency Staff (10 agents, 5-day contract):**

**Projected service level:** 62% → **82%** — 2 points above the 80% target
**Cost:** ~$22,000 (contract rate + NFCU system onboarding overhead)
**Q2 budget impact:** 104% — $2,000 over budget

**Critical risk:** Dynamics 365 provisioning for new external agents takes 24–48 hours. For April 12 coverage, agency staff would need to be contracted and onboarded by April 10 — 3 business days from now. Tight but executable if started today.

**All four scenarios side by side:**
| Scenario | SL | Cost | Budget | Activation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Current plan | 62% | — | 78% | N/A |
| 15 OT shifts | 76% | $18,400 | 96% | 48 hrs |
| Hybrid (OT + cross-train) | 79% | $11,200 | 85% | 24 hrs |
| Agency staff | 82% | $22,000 | 104% | 72 hrs |

**Verdict:** Agency achieves the highest service level but exceeds budget, is slowest to activate, and introduces onboarding risk. The hybrid option is still the strongest choice unless the board has explicitly set 80%+ as a hard minimum.
suggested_chips: Add an agency-rate constraint and re-model
What about the new hire cohort?

confidence.rationale: Scenario sits beyond the modeled volume range — agency-staff productivity ramp is an assumption (no NFCU contracted-agent history this fiscal year). Onboarding risk and budget overrun add downside variance.

nfcu_ana_fourth_scenario_resolved.ai_message:
**Re-modeled with the agency-rate constraint applied.** Workforce Planning supplied a fixed onboarding curve from the FY24 vendor engagement (the only prior contracted-agent comparable). With that constraint anchored:

**Projected service level:** 80% (was 82% — adjusted down for ramp)
**Cost:** $19,800 (was $22,000 — onboarding overhead trimmed)
**Q2 budget impact:** 102% (was 104%)

**The scenario is now defensible.** Confidence has rebounded to 86% because the agency ramp is no longer extrapolated — it's anchored to a real prior engagement. Hybrid is still cheaper and faster, but agency is now a credible fallback if hybrid acceptance falls short.

confidence.rationale: Re-modeled with FY24 vendor engagement as anchor. Onboarding ramp is no longer extrapolated.
